prose:
Fukui electrophilicity (f⁺) analysis on aniline using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard) in PySCF, gas phase, neutral singlet geometry. Charge 0, multiplicity 1; cation and anion states derived with multiplicity 2 each. Partial charges computed via Mulliken (PySCF). The most electrophilic site is C3 (ortho position to the amino group) with f⁺ = 2.298. The most nucleophilic site is N1 (the amino nitrogen) with f⁻ = 0.205. Both the N−1 cation and N+1 anion states converged successfully, with charge conservation checks passing (sum of f⁺ and f⁻ both equal 1.0000). The result is trustworthy. This DFT calculation reveals that aniline's amino group strongly activates the ortho and para positions for electrophilic aromatic substitution, a classic pattern in synthetic organic chemistry.</prose>
